picker.workersRangeError: Concurrent workers must be between {{min}} and {{max}}.
picker.timeoutRangeError: Per-task timeout must be between {{min}} and {{max}} seconds.

evaluate.title: Evaluate
evaluate.subtitle: Pick a skill or plugin and a taskset; an LLM judge scores each task against its rubric
evaluate.errNoSkillTaskset: Select an evaluation target and a taskset before starting.
evaluate.errLimitNegative: The task limit cannot be negative.
evaluate.noSkillMatchPre: No matching skills — head to the
evaluate.tasksetSplit: split · evaluation uses the test split ({{n}} tasks)
evaluate.splitNote: Split tasksets only run the test split during evaluation; use a single-mode taskset to run every task.

train.title: Train
train.subtitle: Optimize one Skill or a complete Plugin; validation gates every accepted candidate
train.errNoSkillTaskset: Select a training target, at least one trainable Skill, and a taskset.
train.errEpochsRange: The number of epochs must be between {{min}} and {{max}}.
train.errLearningRateRange: The learning rate must be between {{min}} and {{max}}.
train.errMaxSkillsRange: Skills updated per candidate must be between 1 and the trainable Skill count.
train.errRegressionRange: Allowed Skill regression must be between 0 and 1.
train.errSplitRatio: The split ratio must be formatted as train:val:test, e.g. 4:3:3.
train.coveragePending: Plugin task coverage is still being checked.
train.coverageLoading: Checking coverage for the selected trainable Skills…
train.noTrainableSupport: This skill has no trainable supporting .md files; only SKILL.md will be trained.
train.singleFileSkill: Single-file skill — no supporting files, so only SKILL.md will be trained.
train.trainableHint: SKILL.md is always the trainable main document (not listed below); checked supporting documents are bundled with it and trained together.
train.trainableSkillsHint: {{selected}} of {{total}} Skills may receive directed edits. Every Skill remains installed during validation.
train.tasksetSingle: single · {{n}} tasks (auto-split by ratio during training)
train.splitRatioNote: A single-mode taskset is deterministically split into train / validation / test groups by this ratio.
train.numEpochsHint: Each epoch runs through the full training set once.
train.gateMetricHint: An edit is accepted only if it strictly improves this metric on the validation set; prefer soft when tasks carry multiple exact specs.
train.learningRateHint: Maximum number of edits accepted per step.
train.maxSkillsHint: Failure attribution chooses at most this many responsible Skills.
train.maxRegressionHint: 0 rejects any covered Skill regression; the Plugin score must still strictly improve.
train.evalTestLabel: Run the test split after training
train.evalTestHint: Enabling it costs an extra round of model calls; leave off for smoke tests.

taskgen.introPre: An AI agent reads the selected skills together and drafts evaluation tasks. The results
taskgen.introPost: : once the job finishes, review it on the detail page and click “Import as new taskset” to confirm in the manual editor before saving.
taskgen.expandIntro: The AI reads the selected skills and the bounded snapshot of every existing split. Results are appended only to the current draft and are not persisted until you save the task set.
taskgen.errNoSkill: Select a skill to generate tasks for first.
taskgen.errNoPluginSkills: Select a plugin and keep at least two skills.
taskgen.errCountRange: The number to generate must be between {{min}} and {{max}}.
taskgen.errTimeoutRange: The timeout must be between {{min}} and {{max}} seconds.
taskgen.errBackendUnavailable: The selected execution backend's CLI is not installed, so it can't be submitted.
taskgen.noPluginMatch: No matching plugin contains multiple skills.
taskgen.noSkillPre: No skills yet — head to the
taskgen.modelPlaceholderCodex: Blank = default model configured in the codex CLI
taskgen.pluginMinimumHint: Plugin minimum: {{perSkill}} tasks per Skill × {{skills}} Skills + {{reserve}} test reserve = {{effective}} tasks generated.
taskgen.guidancePlaceholder: e.g. focus on edge cases; tasks must read input files; increasing difficulty
